steps.title: Identify the critical debate
steps.content: The passage presents two groups of critics. Some fault the abrupt endings of books in the Aubrey/Maturin series, while others argue that the books should be read as parts of one long work rather than as separate novels.

steps.title: Reject distorted purposes
steps.content: Choice A presents a claim about authorial intricacy that the passage does not make. Choice C turns The Forsyte Saga into a prestige comparison, but it is only an analogy. Choice D invents praise for entertainment despite structural flaws. The answer is B.
